January 6, 7992
Mr. Jay Greenwood
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814
Re: Proper Name and Forms for a ControlLed Committee
Which Is Primarily Formed to Oppose the Qualification
and Pas6aqe of a Local Bal,Iot Measure (Reca11)
Dear Jay:
Pursuant to covernment Code section 83114(b)r1 this letter
requests confirmation of the telephone advice you gave me today
regarding the proper committee name and the proper campaign
statenent form for a controlled committee which is primarily
formed to oppose a recall effort in a special district. 
I am
requesting informal assistance as a person with a duty to advj-se
other persons relating to their actions under the Political
Reform Act (2 California Code of Regulations, Section 18329 (c))
Fact s
An officeholder forms and controls a committee for +-he
purpose of opposing the qualification and passage of a recalI
effort against her. The officeholder's name does not appear in
the committee'g name. A1so, the conmittee'B name does not
contain the words " a committee against Proposition (or MeaEure)
The conmittee files its required campaign reports using
EIE-Form 419 (Ba11ot Measure Committee Carnpaign Statement - f.,ong
Questions Presented
1. 
AB a controlled committee, must the corunittee name
include the name of the controlling officeholder?
21 
As a recipient corunittee primarily formed to oppose the
qualification and passage of a local ballot measure 1a reiall),
whose principal activity is to oppose a balIot measure
(a reca1l), must the committee name include the words in this
committee against Proposition (or Measure) _', 
(or
case, "a cornmittee against the recall of 
-',; 
z
1 Al_1
Code, unlessstatutory referenees are
otherwise specified.
to the California Government


Mr. Jay Greenerood
Fair Political Practices Commission
January 6, L992
Page f$ro
3. 
Is the completed Form 419 the
cornmit,tee to use in filing its required proper form for the
campaign reports ?
Discussion
As a controlled committee ( Section 82016 ) !,rhich lras
primarily formed (Section 82047.5) to oppose the qual.if icatj-on
and passage of a Local bal]ot measure (a recall), it rrould seem
that Infornation Manua1 D, in conjunction with the Act and
negul@ 
to determine what the committee 6hou1d
properl-y caLl- itself .
Information Manual D (a1ong with a Form 419) was sent to
me by@ 
of the county in which the reca1l is
pending, to assist in accurately completing the committee,s
reports.
Information Manual D is entitled:
" Campaign Provisions of the Political Reform Act Fori
Recipient Committees Primarily Formed to Support or Oppose
the Oualification or Passage of a State or Local Ballot
Measure (Including Committees Controlled By A Candidate). "
Inf ormation Manua.L D, at page 33, states as follows:
" NAME IDENTIFICATION REQUIREIrIENTS FOR COMI{ITTEES
"Ballot Measure Committees
"In addition to the requirementE for "controlled,,
and "sponsored" recipient committees as aet out beIow,
any conmittee whose principaJ. activity is to support or
oppose a ballot meaaure must include the statement ,,a
committee for Proposit,ion (or Measure) 
or ,'a
cornmj-ttee against proposition 1or tteasu-re 
; _,, 
or
equivalent Language, in any reference to thE-Eofrmittee
under the PoLitical Reform Act. The conmittee,s fuII
name must be used on the conmittee,s Statement of
Organization (Form 410), its campaign statements, and
its mas6 mail j-nqs . . . .


Mr. Jay Greenwood
Fair Political Practices Commission
January 6, L992
Page Three
" ControIl-ed BaIlot Measure Cornmittees
"The name of any committee must include or be
accompanied by the name of any individual, entity, or
other pereon by which the committee i6 controlled.... "
According
Uglllgl_e, the
should contain
6houId contain
to the above information set forth in Information
name of the committee deecribed in the FEEEs- aEove
the name of the controlling officeholdET-E-nd
the words, "a commj-ttee against the recall of
See alao Section 84107.
when I asked you if the conunittee is considered both a
controlled committae and a primarily formed committee:nd- if both
of these name requirements were in effect, you replied that the
committee should not be considered both a controlled cornmittee
and a primarily formed comrnittee, but shouLd be considered on!1 a
controLled corunittee. Accordingly, you stated that the wordETa
committee against the recalL of
" would not be required.
You further stated that the requirenent that a controlled
committee's name include the name of the controlling officeholder
is no longer in effect, as a result of Proposition 68 being held
inapplicable.
Based on your advice, therefore, the name of the conmittee
described in the Facts above would not need to include the
controlling officEEol[Zier's name or EEE word6 "a conunittee against
the recall of
A6
Manual Dto the proper campaign statement form to use,
state6, on the back of the cover page, that:
I nformation
"A controlled committee 'primarily formed. to
support or oppose a local ba11ot measure or
the qualification of-E--I6ca t ballot measure
must f il,e campaign di s cI6EIie reports at the
same times and in the 6ame location(s) that
the controlling candidate or officeholder
must file his or her regular semi-annual and
pre-election campaign reporta as specified in
Information Manual A. In addition, the
committee must file reports as a ba1lot
measure conmittee and must folLow the
instructions in Information Manual D.
qgllqt Measure Committees should file
not Form 490" (emphasis added).


Mr. Jay Greenwood
Fair Po]itical Practices Commission
January 6, L992
Page Four
According to the Regi6trar,/C1erk, and as you mentioned, the
committee should file its campaign statements on the Form 419,
and not the Form 420.
Pl-ea6e confirm that my understanding of our conversation and
your advice vra6 correct. If not, please advise me otherwise, 60
that I may advise my client a6 to the proper name for the
committee and the proper canpaign statements to fil,e.
Thank you for your attention to this matter.
very truly yours,
B""$rrD€S
